subject: [PATCH v3 0/4] mm: improve large folio readahead and alignment for exec memory

reviews.author: Rui
reviews.summary: Identified a double-put bug in fuse_try_move_page() where the folio refcount drops to zero prematurely. Suggested adding an extra folio_get() before replace_page_cache_folio() to maintain the reference.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Suggested disabling the mmap_miss logic for VM_EXEC to prevent silent readahead disablement after 100 page faults.

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand
reviews.summary: Suggested taking into account zone high watermarks in the exec_folio_order() function to improve readahead alignment.

2026-04-02.patch_summary: This patch series aims to improve large folio readahead and alignment for executable memory by addressing issues in the existing exec_folio_order() function. The main problems are that the mmap_miss heuristic silently disables exec readahead after 100 page faults, and the function does not take into account zone high watermarks or align properly with segment VA and file offset. The patches propose changes to handle these issues, including disabling the mmap_miss logic for VM_EXEC, aligning in elf only when segment VA and file offset are already aligned, and extending max alignment to mapping_max_folio_size().
